title: viola heterophylla subsp. cavillieri
intro: Viola heterophylla subsp. cavillieri, also known as the Alpine Viola or Viola cavillieri, is a plant species belonging to the Violet family (Violaceae). It is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and has been traditionally used for its medicinal and culinary properties. The plant is known for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been used to treat various health issues, including wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. The herbal preparations of Viola heterophylla subsp. cavillieri include infusions, tinctures, and topical creams, which can be used to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory conditions such as bronchitis and asthma.
